sanskrit:
ज्ञेयः स नित्यसंन्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्क्षति।
निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते॥
transliteration: jïeyaù sa nitya-sannyäsé yo na dveñöi na käìkñati | nirdvandvo hi mahä-bäho sukhaà bandhät pramucyate ||
literal_meaning: Know that one as the eternal renouncer who neither hates nor hankers; free from all opposites, O mighty-armed, he easily is released from bondage.
contextual_meaning: True renunciation is not external but an internal stance of non-reactivity—neither pushing away nor clinging to experience. When the mind drops its tug-of-war with pleasure and pain, the nervous system exits survival mode and “bondage” (chronic stress) dissolves.
psychological_state: Secure-attachment to Self: the prefrontal cortex is online, the threat system is offline, and the default-mode network stops compulsive planning.
trigger_scenario: You refresh your email expecting a promotion letter; instead there’s a lay-off notice. Your heart races, but you notice the urge to either rage or catastrophize, pause, breathe, and let the email be data—not a verdict on your worth.
anchor_text: Let go of push and pull; rest in calm presence.
